title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. triphyllos
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. triphyllos, also known as the water crowfoot, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. triphyllos include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its therapeutic properties.
